my_posts: I was lonely for years
I spent years without friends, no one to share how I feel, think, or plan with. It was tough. I doubted myself, felt like an outsider, and let relationships fade

For me, loneliness isn’t just being alone, it’s feeling empty and unable to connect. It lasted for years, and honestly, it wasn’t worth it. I was moody, frustrated, never satisfied. I joked about it till I accepted it
I guess the message is that friends matter. Cherish them, don’t take them for granted. And if you feel lonely, don’t romanticize it, find people, find communities, don’t fight your instincts
I can’t glamorize it. It was a fail, the cons always outweighed the pros. But over time, loneliness did make me OK with being judged, more self-aware, and appreciate people in my life
If you get this far, thank you ❤️🦜
Remember me... (Coco quote)
Don’t forget your family, don’t forget you were their child before anything else
If Medgule was ready to leave his dream for his family, you can leave the grinding and spend time with your family
Whoever you call yourself, those people were behind it all, and they never ask for equity or titles
If you got this far, thank you ❤️ 🦜
P.S. If you live with your family, you are privileged, use it wisely. If not, a reminder to visit your family
Don't Try
Chasing happiness makes you feel unhappy
Chasing productivity makes you feel unproductive
Grinding nonstop makes you feel like it’s never enough (tho rest makes you value your work)
Trying to look cool just proves you’re ordinary
Seeking something only confirms you don’t have it
Real happiness comes from caring less about the noise and more about what matters
Care less, but care better
If you got this far, thank you ❤️ 🦜
P.S. My account got restricted because I built something that apparently broke LinkedIn privacy :-) (I'm not trying again)
You Are Not Special
If everyone’s unique, then no one is
That just makes us all… normal.
My problems, my family, my name,
none of it’s special, someone else has it too
And that’s fine
Because being normal is what makes us real
Even the people we call “special”
just want to live like everyone else
It's amazing to understand what is accually normal
So stop chasing unique
Own normal
That’s the real flex
And yes, of course there are some ninja elsewhere in the world,
I may not have gotten the chance to meet them yet
Hard Work Isn’t Enough
Most people ignore this truth
Mark Zuckerberg, Steve Jobs, Sam Altman and many others had advantages
Ivy League schools, supportive families, or growing up in opportunity-rich places like the US
Hard work mattered, but privilege played a huge role
It is easy to say work harder, but hard to admit they had what you do not
Compare fairly, success should be measured by what is possible for you, not someone else
If you made it this far, thank you 🦜
YOU CAN’T BE A LOSER
Procrastination is just a skill you haven’t built yet
It’s not cuz you’re bad, not cuz you’re lazy. It’s just something you can get better at (or worse, depending on how you see it)
If your phone’s the problem, move it. If the task sucks, switch it or add something fun to it
And don’t fall for the “strict 50 habits routine” or trying to do “40h of work in a day”
That just makes it worse and makes you more likely to procrastinate.
Self-loathing won’t help. This isn’t a 1-day fix, it’s a skill you build with small steps
Don’t be harsh on yourself. Treat yourself like a friend, not an enemy
If you made it this far, thank you 🦜
